name: Impossible Cake
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Generously grease a 6-inch fluted tube pan such as Bundt with butter or cooking spray, then lightly dust with some All-Purpose Flour, tapping to remove excess. Fill a shallow baking pan with enough Water to come one-third up the sides of the tube pan. Place the water bath in the oven to warm up while it preheats.

2. Pour the Caramel Sauce into a microwave-safe bowl. Microwave on high until warm and easily pourable, about 1 minute. Pour into the prepared tube pan, tilting to completely cover the bottom in an even layer.

3. In a medium bowl, whisk together the remaining All-Purpose Flour, Cocoa Powder, Baking Soda, and Baking Powder until well combined.

4. In a large bowl, beat the Butter with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth and creamy. Add the White Sugar and beat until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes. Beat in one Egg and the Brewed Coffee until incorporated. Add the flour mixture in three additions, alternating with the Whole Milk in two additions, mixing on low speed just until combined after each addition. Pour the chocolate cake batter over the caramel layer in the tube pan.

5. In the bowl of a food processor, combine the Evaporated Milk, Condensed Milk, the remaining two Eggs, and Vanilla Extract. Pulse until completely smooth and well blended. Carefully pour this flan mixture over the cake batter through a fine-mesh sieve to catch any lumps, creating two distinct layers.

6. Carefully place the filled tube pan into the water bath in the preheated oven. Bake until a toothpick inserted into the cake layer comes out clean and the flan is set, about 1 hour. The layers will magically switch positions during baking.

7. Remove from the oven and cool on a wire rack for about 1 hour, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours. To unmold, carefully run a thin knife around all edges to loosen, tap the pan firmly a few times, then shake gently. Place a rimmed serving plate over the pan and quickly invert to release the cake with caramel cascading over the top.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven and prepare a 6-inch tube pan with flour dusting and warm Caramel Sauce on the bottom.

2. Make chocolate cake batter with some All-Purpose Flour, Cocoa Powder, one Egg, Butter, and other dry ingredients.

3. Prepare flan mixture by blending Evaporated Milk, Condensed Milk, two Eggs, and Vanilla Extract.

4. Layer the batters in the pan and bake in a water bath until set.

5. Cool completely, refrigerate, then invert onto a plate to reveal the impossible layers.
